title: How to automate task status updates based on a specific date?

posts.author: makis34693 (makis)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone…

I am trying to set up an automation that will update the status of a task in my board based on a specific date. I have a column that tracks the due date of tasks, and I had like the status to change automatically when that due date is reached.

I have explored the automation options available, but I’m not sure if I am missing something or if it’s just not possible with the current options. I want to make the status switch from “In Progress” to “Completed” when the due date arrives, but I am not sure how to trigger this in a way that doesn’t require manual intervention.

I also check this: https://community.monday.com/t/automating-task-dependencies-in-monday-com-auto-update-status-and-starawsdevopstraining But I have not found any solution. Could anyone guide me about this? I’ve tried setting up a simple “when date arrives, change status” automation, but it seems like it doesn’t quite work the way I expected. Does anyone have any experience with this kind of automation or can suggest an approach that would work? Also, if there are any potential limitations I should be aware of when automating based on date, that would be really helpful to know as well.

Thanks in advance for any advice or tips!

Respected community member!

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: The “when date arrives, change status” automation is what I’d recommend. What’s not working with it?
